sections.title: Executive Summary
sections.rendered_content:
This comparison evaluates AMG, CEG across their locked annual filing periods, examining revenue scale, profitability, leverage, and cash generation to surface relative positioning within the peer set.

AMG reports $2.07B in revenue (1.6% YoY) and $717M in net income for period 2025-12-31. Key profile markers include net margin of 34.5%, debt-to-equity of 0.64x, free cash flow of $967M.

CEG reports $22.7B in revenue (19.5% YoY) and $2.32B in net income for period 2025-12-31. Key profile markers include net margin of 10.2%, debt-to-equity of 0.49x, free cash flow of $1.29B.

CEG leads the peer set in absolute revenue scale, while AMG carries the highest net margin, illustrating a scale-versus-efficiency tradeoff in the current locked basis.

sections.title: Relative Strengths
sections.rendered_content:
### AMG — What Stands Out
- ROE of 16.9% indicates efficient use of shareholder capital.

### CEG — What Stands Out
- ROE of 16.3% indicates efficient use of shareholder capital.

- Revenue is up 19.5% year over year, showing strong top-line momentum.
- Current ratio of 1.53x indicates solid liquidity.

sections.title: Risk Comparison
sections.rendered_content:
### Watch Items
- Relative risk is assessed only on surfaced, period-locked peer metrics and does not force false comparability.

- **AMG:** Significant spike: 19.0% YoY change (2.1σ from mean) (period: 2021-12-31) Significant spike: 12.5% YoY change (2.0σ from mean) (period: 2021-12-31)
- **CEG:** No major quantitative red flag is active in the current annual snapshot.

sections.title: Analyst Notes
sections.rendered_content:
### Analyst Follow-Up
- Peer conclusions should be read through the locked annual basis, surfaced metric coverage, and explicit comparison-governance policy.

- **AMG:** 1 strength signals and 2 active risk signals in the locked annual basis.
- **CEG:** 3 strength signals and 0 active risk signals in the locked annual basis.
- Prioritize next-pass work on margin durability, balance-sheet flexibility, and cash conversion differentials across the peer set.
